shared.label: Legal information
shared.draftBody: These pages are prepared for general information purposes about NoraMedi's data processing practices. They do not constitute legal advice. Clinics and other data controllers should consult a legal adviser for their own obligations.
shared.sourcesDescription: The following official legislation and authority publications were considered when defining the scope of this page.

center.title: Legal Information Centre
center.description: General information pages on privacy, cookies, communication channels and data subject rights applicable to the NoraMedi platform.
center.pagesDescription: Each page explains NoraMedi's relevant data processing practices and the applicable regulatory framework.

scope.title: Scope and limitation
scope.body: NoraMedi is a SaaS/CRM platform for managing clinic operations, provided by Mustafa Basol - Peaknova. Patient, appointment and communications flows may contain personal data and, in some uses, special category health-related data, so data minimisation and authorised access are required. NoraMedi is not positioned as a diagnosis, prescription or full electronic health record platform.

checklist.title: Data protection guidance for clinics
checklist.description: Clinics using the platform for their own patient and communications data should assess the following obligations in their capacity as data controllers.
items: The clinic must prepare its own patient-facing notice and, where required, explicit consent documentation.
Actual data categories, purposes and retention periods must be matched to the clinic-level data inventory.
Before WhatsApp/Instagram channel features are enabled, clinic-level processor roles and transfer conditions must be documented.
Approval/opt-out mechanisms and, where required, IYS processes for commercial electronic communications are the clinic's responsibility.
The exclusion of special category health data from message channels and access controls must be operationally verified.

sources.kvkk: Personal Data Protection Authority - KVKK information
sources.commercial: Republic of Türkiye Ministry of Trade - Electronic commerce legislation

privacy.title: Privacy and Personal Data Processing Notice
privacy.description: General information on personal data processing, transfers, retention and data subject rights under KVKK and, where applicable, GDPR, within the NoraMedi platform.

controller.title: 1. Platform provider and contact
controller.body: NoraMedi is a SaaS clinic operations management platform provided by Mustafa Basol - Peaknova. Clinics act as the primary data controller under KVKK and, where applicable, GDPR for processing arising from their own patient, appointment and communications data. NoraMedi may act as a data processor or service provider within the platform and infrastructure services framework; this role is determined separately according to the service agreement and usage context.

data.title: 2. Data categories that may be processed
data.body: The following scope is based on the platform's current features and clinic usage scenarios. Clinics should determine their specific processing scope against their own data inventory.

rows.label: Public web use and functional preferences
rows.value: Functional browser storage data such as language and theme preferences; no analytics or advertising tracking tool is used in this version.

rows.label: Demo / contact requests
rows.value: If a demo or contact form is enabled, submitted contact information may be processed.

rows.label: Platform account and user data
rows.value: Username, email, encrypted authentication credentials, role and permission data, sign-in/sign-out records.

rows.label: Clinic / organisation / branch configuration data
rows.value: Clinic name, branch details, working hours, service definitions and organisational structure.

rows.label: Staff roles and permission data
rows.value: Roles assigned to clinic staff, access permissions and operational task information.

rows.label: Patient contact and appointment data
rows.value: Patient name, contact details, appointment and follow-up data processed in the CRM. Health-related data is classified as special category data.

rows.label: WhatsApp / Instagram connection metadata and message content
rows.value: When a channel is enabled, connection details, sender identifiers, message date, content and conversation status may be processed.

rows.label: AI assistant processing data
rows.value: When AI-assisted features are enabled, conversation context and appointment-matching data may be sent to the AI provider.

rows.label: Security / audit / operational logs
rows.value: Authorisation, activity and audit records kept for access control and incident investigation.

rows.label: Billing / payment tracking data
rows.value: Invoice and transaction data related to platform subscriptions and CRM payment tracking.

purposes.title: 3. Purposes of processing
items: Operating the CRM platform and providing clinic, branch, team and role-based operations management.
Account creation, authentication, security and access management.
Managing appointments, follow-up tasks, treatment/service workflows and payment visibility.
WhatsApp and Instagram communications channel routing and inbox management (when a channel is enabled).
AI-assisted appointment matching and communications assistant function (when the feature is enabled).
Support, troubleshooting, audit and abuse prevention.
Fulfilling legal and contractual obligations.
Responding to data subject requests.

basis.title: 4. Legal bases and sensitive data
basis.body: The legal basis for each data category must be determined separately according to actual usage, the controller's role and the targeted country. Consent must not be treated as a general assumed basis where another legal basis would be required.

rows.label: Service and account operations
rows.value: Appropriate bases such as performance of a contract, legal obligation or, where conditions are met, legitimate interests are assessed under KVKK Article 5 and GDPR Article 6.

rows.label: Security and audit
rows.value: The basis of legal obligation or proportionate legitimate interests for authorised access and incident management is documented by a concrete risk assessment.

rows.label: Marketing communications
rows.value: No message is sent before the required permission/objection mechanism is established under Turkish commercial electronic communications rules and IYS where required, or ePrivacy, GDPR and relevant member state rules for EU audiences.

basis.special: Health data is special category personal data under KVKK and special category data under GDPR. It may be processed only where genuinely necessary, with an applicable special processing condition determined and suitable technical and organisational controls and restricted access; it must not be used in marketing messages.

transfers.title: 5. Recipients and international transfers
transfers.body: NoraMedi may transfer personal data to recipients in the following categories in order to provide its services. Specific recipients may vary according to the service agreement and clinic configuration.
recipients: Hosting and infrastructure providers (servers, databases, file storage).
Email service provider (for notification, invitation and password-reset messages).
Meta Platforms / WhatsApp / Instagram (when channel features are enabled by the clinic).
Google / Gemini or the relevant AI provider (when AI-assisted features are enabled).
Legal and administrative authorities (when required by law or upon authorised request).
Clinic staff and authorised organisation users (within role-based access controls).
transfers.international: Some infrastructure and service providers may process personal data outside Turkey or the EU. Such transfers are assessed under KVKK Article 9 and, where applicable, GDPR Chapter V. International transfer conditions may vary according to clinic configuration and enabled features.

retention.title: 6. Retention and deletion
retention.body: Data is kept only as long as required for its purpose. The following retention periods apply at platform level; clinics may define additional policies in line with their own data obligations.

rows.label: Public site preferences
rows.value: Stored in the browser as functional preferences and removable by the user.

rows.label: Conversation messages
rows.value: 365 days (subject to the automated clean-up policy).

rows.label: Conversation state
rows.value: 90 days.

rows.label: Inbound channel events
rows.value: 90 days.

rows.label: Operational events
rows.value: 180 days.

rows.label: Contact / demo requests
rows.value: 365 days.

rows.label: Old resolved inbox entries
rows.value: May be subject to redaction or masking under the retention policy.

rows.label: Legal, audit, financial, clinical and core patient records
rows.value: Not deleted automatically by the support-data clean-up job; subject to applicable legal and contractual retention periods.

rights.title: 7. Data subject rights and requests
rights.body: Depending on scope, data subjects may exercise rights under KVKK Article 11 and GDPR Articles 15-22. These rights include:
items: Learn whether their data is processed and request information and access.
Request correction of incomplete or inaccurate data.
Request deletion, destruction or anonymisation where applicable conditions are met.
Request restriction of processing or object to processing.
Learn the third parties to which data has been transferred and request data portability where appropriate.
Withdraw consent for consent-based processing.
Lodge a complaint with the competent supervisory authority.
rights.contact: Privacy requests relating to the NoraMedi platform and account data should be sent to privacy@noramedi.com; security reports to security@noramedi.com; support and account access queries to support@noramedi.com; and legal or contractual notices to legal@noramedi.com. Requests relating to clinic patient data should be directed primarily to the relevant clinic (the data controller); NoraMedi may assist the clinic where contractually applicable.

sources.notice: KVKK - Guide on Fulfilling the Information Obligation
sources.transfer: KVKK - Announcement on standard contracts for international transfers (10.07.2024)
sources.gdpr: EUR-Lex - GDPR (particularly Articles 9, 13-14 and Chapter V)

cookies.title: Cookies and Local Storage Policy
cookies.description: Information on the current position and use rules for browser storage methods used in the public marketing page and signed-in product area.

current.title: Current public page position
current.body: Based on a code review, the `/landing` page contains no advertising, analytics pixel or marketing cookie. The application includes local storage behaviour for interface preferences; the signed-in product area may additionally use functional data such as session and clinic selection.

scope.title: 1. What does this policy explain?
scope.body: Cookies and similar browser storage methods may be used to store information on a device or access stored information. Where this constitutes personal data processing, data protection duties under KVKK apply; for EU users, ePrivacy rules and, where personal data is involved, GDPR provisions must also be assessed.

storage.title: 2. Current functional storage use
storage.body: The table below describes functional storage areas observed in the current application code. It must be verified again before production deployment and before adding third-party tools.

rows.label: Theme preference
rows.value: Read from local storage to apply the interface light/dark appearance preference. It may remain until the user clears it through browser settings.

rows.label: Language preference
rows.value: The i18next language detection configuration uses local storage to remember the interface language. This is functional use.

rows.label: Signed-in product area
rows.value: After sign-in, functional local storage values may be used to run the session, provide authorised views and maintain the selected clinic preference; they are not necessary for public marketing browsing.

future.title: 3. If optional tools are added
future.body: If analytics, advertising, retargeting or similar non-essential tools are added in future, this current policy alone will not be sufficient.
items: Before tools are enabled, their name, provider, purpose, duration, recipient and any international transfer must be added to the inventory and this page.
Where required, non-essential storage or access must not begin before obtaining a user's choice; rejecting must be as easy as accepting.
A management surface for later preference changes and consent records must be prepared.
If third-party tools process personal data, the Privacy Notice must be updated at the same time.

controls.title: 4. Preferences and questions
items: Users can clear existing local storage data through browser settings; this may reset language, theme or session behaviour.
If non-essential cookies are introduced, this page will provide a link to access the preference panel.
For questions on data processing or browser storage, please contact contact@noramedi.com.

sources.edpb: EDPB - Guidelines 2/2023 on the technical scope of Article 5(3), final version (16.10.2024)

communications.title: Communication Channels and Integrations Notice
communications.description: Data protection, content principles and security information relating to the use of WhatsApp, Instagram and AI assistant features in NoraMedi clinic operations.

scope.title: 1. About channel features
scope.body: WhatsApp and Instagram connection features and the AI-assisted appointment assistant are optional features in NoraMedi that must be configured by the clinic. When these features are enabled, the relevant clinic must complete its own patient/contact notice and, where required, its explicit consent process before collecting patient and communications data.

channels.title: 2. Supported channel flows
channels.body: When a channel is enabled, authorised clinic users can manage communications requests through the following flows.

rows.label: WhatsApp
rows.value: Association of conversations with clinic operations through connection management and an inbox.

rows.label: Instagram DM
rows.value: Routing of requests from the DM inbox into appointment flows.

rows.label: Meta and platform roles
rows.value: Meta Platforms, WhatsApp and Instagram are independent data controllers/service providers for their own platforms. User data on those platforms is subject to Meta's privacy terms.

rows.label: Personal data in message content
rows.value: If users choose to share personal information in channel messages, that information becomes part of the message content. Sensitive health information must not be shared in messages.

roles.title: 3. Controller responsibility
roles.body: The clinic acts as the primary data controller to the extent it determines purposes for its patients and communications requests. Whether NoraMedi acts as a data processor or independent controller is determined in the service agreement. Meta, WhatsApp and Instagram are independent data controllers/service providers for their own platforms.

ai.title: 4. AI assistant feature
ai.body: When the AI-assisted appointment assistant is enabled, conversation context may be processed by the relevant AI provider. Key limitations of this feature:
items: The AI assistant does not provide diagnosis, treatment recommendations, emergency health assessments or medical advice of any kind.
The AI assistant operates solely for appointment matching and communications routing purposes.
The AI assistant feature must be enabled by the clinic and appropriate patient information must be provided.

messages.title: 5. Separate operational messages from marketing communications
messages.body: A channel being technically ready does not mean every type of message can be sent without permission.

rows.label: Appointment / operational information
rows.value: Assessed as necessary and proportionate service communication. A message should carry minimum content, such as an appointment reminder only, and must not contain diagnosis, treatment detail or sensitive health information.

rows.label: Campaign or promotion
rows.value: In Turkey, no message is sent before completing the processes required by Law No. 6563, Commercial Electronic Communication rules and IYS where applicable.

rows.label: Direct marketing to EU recipients
rows.value: No message is sent without establishing the permission or objection mechanism required after assessment of ePrivacy rules, GDPR and the implementation rules of the target member state.

protection.title: 6. Data minimisation and security
items: No diagnosis, treatment plan, image, prescription or other sensitive health content should be sent in a WhatsApp or Instagram message body.
Incoming messages should only be viewed by roles that need access for their duties, and access logs should be kept.
When an appointment request is converted to an operational record, only necessary information should be transferred; unnecessary conversation content should not be duplicated.
An incident response process must exist for messages sent to the wrong recipient, account takeover or unauthorised access.

emergency.title: Emergency health situations
emergency.body: NoraMedi and its channel features are not designed for emergency health services. In an urgent or life-threatening health situation, please contact emergency services (112 in Turkey) or the relevant emergency healthcare provider directly. Do not wait for a response through clinic appointment channels.

sources.commercial: Republic of Türkiye Ministry of Trade - Electronic commerce legislation and Commercial Electronic Communications rules
sources.transfer: KVKK - Official documents on international transfers of personal data
sources.eprivacy: EUR-Lex - ePrivacy Directive 2002/58/EC (Article 13 for direct marketing)

dataRequest.title: Data Subject Request
dataRequest.description: Find out how to submit an access, correction, deletion, objection or complaint request regarding your personal data.

platform.title: 1. NoraMedi platform requests
platform.body: For requests relating to your NoraMedi platform account, privacy preferences or data processed by the platform, please use the following contact addresses.

rows.label: Privacy / KVKK / GDPR requests
rows.value: privacy@noramedi.com

rows.label: Security reports and suspected breaches
rows.value: security@noramedi.com

rows.label: Account access and support queries
rows.value: support@noramedi.com

rows.label: Legal and contractual notices
rows.value: legal@noramedi.com

types.title: 2. Request categories
types.body: You may exercise the following rights under KVKK Article 11 and, where applicable, GDPR Articles 15-22:
items: Access and information: Learn whether your data is processed and obtain a copy.
Correction: Request correction of incomplete or inaccurate data.
Deletion or anonymisation: Request deletion or anonymisation of your data where applicable conditions are met.
Objection or restriction: Object to processing or request its restriction.
Withdrawal of consent: Withdraw consent for consent-based processing.
Transfer information: Learn the third parties to which your data has been transferred.
Security and privacy complaint: Report a suspected data breach or security vulnerability.
Formal complaint: Lodge a complaint with the KVKK or the competent EU supervisory authority.

clinic.title: 3. Clinic patient data requests
clinic.body: Patient records, appointment data and clinical communications data processed by a clinic on the NoraMedi platform are primarily controlled by that clinic. You should direct requests relating to such data to the clinic from which you received services. NoraMedi may assist the clinic where the contractual framework allows; however, NoraMedi does not make primary controller decisions on behalf of clinics.

response.title: 4. Response timelines
response.body: Requests are aimed to be answered within thirty days under KVKK and within one month under GDPR. This period may be extended depending on the nature or scope of the request; you will be informed in such cases. Submitting your request in writing to privacy@noramedi.com will help to speed up the process.

sources.kvkk: Personal Data Protection Authority - Data subject rights and application procedures

consent.title: Channel Consent Templates
consent.description: Sample notice and explicit consent wording for WhatsApp and Instagram appointment channels. The texts on this page are general templates for clinics to adapt to their own conditions.

disclaimer.title: About the template nature of this page
disclaimer.body: The texts on this page are general templates and do not constitute a guarantee of legal compliance. Each clinic should review these texts with a legal adviser in light of its own services, processed data categories and applicable law.

distinction.title: 1. Distinguishing notice from explicit consent
distinction.body: Under KVKK and GDPR, the obligation to provide a notice (aydınlatma) and explicit consent are distinct concepts and must be fulfilled separately.
items: Notice: Covers the identity of the controller, processing purposes, legal bases, retention periods, transfer recipients and data subject rights. Must be provided independently of whether consent is obtained.

Explicit consent: Required only for processing operations where explicit consent is the identified legal basis. Must be obtained separately, freely, specifically and in an informed manner for each processing operation.

For appointment operations, a different legal basis such as performance of a contract or legitimate interests may also be considered; in such cases, a notice alone may be sufficient without explicit consent.

template.title: 2. Sample wording for appointment channels
template.body: The following texts are sample templates for channels receiving appointment requests via WhatsApp or Instagram. They should be adapted by the clinic to include the clinic name, controller details and notice link.
template.notice: Sample notice link: "To learn how your personal data is processed, you may read the [Clinic Name] Privacy Notice: [link]"
template.explicit: Sample explicit consent: "I consent to the processing of my personal data for the purpose of receiving my appointment request and being contacted in response."

clinicNote.title: 3. Clinic adaptation requirement
clinicNote.body: These templates provide only a general starting point. Each clinic is responsible for preparing its own notice including the controller's business name, data categories processed, retention periods and transfer conditions, and where required its explicit consent documentation, for its patient communications channel. Using these templates without modification does not guarantee legal compliance.

sources.kvkk: KVKK - Guide on Fulfilling the Information Obligation
sources.gdpr: EUR-Lex - GDPR Article 7 and Recital 32 (conditions for consent)
